title: Assam govt renames Guwahati roads; GS Road is Srimanta Sankardev Path - The News Mill
content: The Assam government on May 4 renamed major roads in Guwahati. In a press conference, Assam minister Himanta Biswa Sarma announced that the government has renamed the GS Road as Srimanta Sankardev Path, AT Road as Damodardev Path, VIP Road as Madhabdev Path, Hatigaon Road as Haridev Path and the Guwahati Club Circle as Lakshminath Bezbaruah Chowk. He added that the step was taken as the old names were given during the British era. The stretch from Six Mile to Narengi VIP Road would now be known as Mahapurush Madhabdev Path, Ganesh Mandir to NH via Hatigaon as Mahapurush Haridev Path, Ganesh Mandir via Kahilipara to Lalganesh as Pandit Deendayal Upadhyaya Path, Kharghuli to Noonmati as Dr Bhupen Hazarika Path, Hengrabari Road to Chachal VIP Point as Khagen Mahanta Path, Guwahati Club Traffic Rotary as Sahityarathi Lakshminath Bezbaroa Chowk, Sijubari Path as Chaolung Sukapha Path and the Beltola Khanapara Road as Pir Ajan Fakir Path. This comes after the news that the state government was exploring if traditional attire – dhoti and kurta for male and Mekhela Chadar for female – can be made optional in government offices on certain days. Home » Northeast India » Assam » Assam govt renames Guwahati roads; GS Road is Srimanta Sankardev Path The News Mill is a Guwahati-based digital media with focus on content from across Northeast India and beyond. It starts from the book value of the business and compares the returns on that equity to the cost of equity. For Goldman Sachs Group, book value is estimated at $356.47 per share, with a stable book value of $402.19 per share based on weighted future book value estimates from 14 analysts. What counts as a “normal” or “fair” P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth prospects and risk. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while lower growth or higher risk usually aligns with a lower multiple. Goldman Sachs Group currently trades on a P/E of 16.29x. That is below the Capital Markets industry average of 36.13x and below the peer group average of 24.80x. title: Air Jordan 4 GS "Black Infrared 23" Releases May 2026 - Sneaker Files
content: The Air Jordan 4 GS “Black Infrared 23” will release on May 1, 2026. A grade-school exclusive colorway built for younger sneakerheads, this pair expands the Air Jordan 4’s already packed 2026 lineup. The retail price is $165. Air Jordan 4 GS “Infrared 23”Color: Black/Bright Mango-Barely Volt-Infrared 23Release Date: May 1, 2026Style Code: II1271-001Price: $165Where to Buy: Nike, Foot Locker, DSG, Finish Line, Hibbett, ShiekhMore Info: Air Jordan Release Dates Jordan Brand’s Summer 2026 calendar keeps delivering, and this next one is for the kids, though plenty of adults are going to want it in their size, too. Known as the “Black Infrared 23” Air Jordan 4, the look doesn’t scream “kids’ exclusive,” which is why I feel the brand should have dropped these in adult sizes as well. Jordan Brand has made a clear effort this year to give younger collectors their own chapter of the Air Jordan 4 story, and this new colorway should be a hit. This Air Jordan 4 is dressed in a Black, Bright Mango, Barely Volt, and Infrared 23 color combination. Highlighted with a Black suede upper. Jordan Brand adds energy with Barely Volt accents on the mesh netting and tongue, making the shoe feel more vibrant. The real standout comes lower on the shoe, where Bright Mango and Infrared 23 work together across the midsole and outsole. Furthermore, the midsole has speckled details. Lastly, Jumpman branding is throughout, while the tongue label features water droplets. The Air Jordan 4 GS “Black Infrared 23” will be released on May 1st, 2026, at select Jordan Brand retailers, including Nike, Foot Locker, DSG, Finish Line, Hibbett, and Shiekh. The retail price is $165 in grade school sizes. Below, you can check out more photos for a closer look. This pair will stand out for sure, and since I can see some adults wanting to pick it up, it’s a pass for me. The colorway is a bit vibrant for my taste as of late, although I do like it when the brand uses Infrared. I think the addition of Barely Volt and Mango is what throws it off. But if you like them, don’t let me talk you out of buying. No, this colorway was released exclusively in kids’ sizes. It’s a great option for younger sneaker fans or anyone looking for a versatile, everyday Jordan 4 with a bit of color pop. Your email address will not be published. title: GS Caltex signs strategic partnership with Veolia to enhance utilities and water treatment - chemengonline.com
content:
Please contact clientservices@accessintel.com
		or call 1-888-707-5814 if you are unable to login. GS Caltex (Seoul, South Korea) ) announced that it had signed a comprehensive memorandum of understanding (MOU) with Veolia (Paris, France), a global environmental solutions company,  to innovate utility operations and strengthen sustainability at its Yeosu Complex. The two companies signed the MOU following the visit of French President Emmanuel Macron to Korea, expanding their relationship from a limited, project-based collaboration in water treatment to a broader partnership aimed at sharing future strategies. GS Caltex had signed a comprehensive memorandum of understanding (MOU) with Veolia to innovate utility operations and strengthen sustainability at its Yeosu Complex. (From left: GS Caltex CEO Saehong Hur; Veolia Senior Executive Vice President of Business Performance Christophe Maquet) (source: GS Caltex) To address tightening environmental regulations and growing demands for energy efficiency, the partnership will focus on developing and implementing integrated solutions across environmental, energy, and digital sectors. This will be pursued through the Triple-X framework, encompassing Business Transformation (BX), Digital & AI Transformation (DAX), and Green Transformation (GX). Under BX, the partnership will explore ways to integrate and optimize wastewater treatment operations at the Yeosu Complex, as well as opportunities to enhance utility synergies with nearby companies. These efforts are expected to maximize operational efficiency and bolster competitiveness. DAX, a key strategic priority for GS Caltex, will see the introduction of Veolia’s AI-based digital solutions at the Yeosu Complex to refine real-time analysis of utility operations data and improve intelligent process management systems. By leveraging these capabilities, GS Caltex will accelerate the transition to a smart plant through process optimization, anomaly detection, and predictive maintenance. For GX, focused on sustainability, GS Caltex will collaborate with Veolia to adopt the latter’s proprietary eco-friendly technologies at the Yeosu Complex, including wastewater reuse, cooling system upgrades, and resource recovery. The approach is intended to reduce carbon emissions and solidify the foundation for a circular economy. CEO Saehong Hur said, “Our collaboration with Veolia will serve as a catalyst for transforming our overall utility operations and elevating our ESG management to the next level,” adding, “We will strengthen our sustainable competitiveness based on world-class technology and operational expertise.” Chemical Engineering news and content delivered every week © Access Intelligence, LLC.

title: Why Goldman Sachs (GS) is a Top Dividend Stock for Your Portfolio - Yahoo! Finance Canada
content: Tip: Try a valid symbol or a specific company name for relevant results Getting big returns from financial portfolios, whether through stocks, bonds, ETFs, other securities, or a combination of all, is an investor's dream. But when you're an income investor, your primary focus is generating consistent cash flow from each of your liquid investments. While cash flow can come from bond interest or interest from other types of investments, income investors hone in on dividends. A dividend is the distribution of a company's earnings paid out to shareholders; it's often viewed by its dividend yield, a metric that measures a dividend as a percent of the current stock price. Many academic studies show that dividends account for significant portions of long-term returns, with dividend contributions exceeding one-third of total returns in many cases. Goldman Sachs (GS) is headquartered in New York, and is in the Finance sector. The stock has seen a price change of -1.82% since the start of the year. The investment bank is currently shelling out a dividend of $4.50 per share, with a dividend yield of 2.09%. This compares to the Financial - Investment Bank industry's yield of 1.2% and the S&P 500's yield of 1.47%. Looking at dividend growth, the company's current annualized dividend of $18.00 is up 28.6% from last year. Over the last 5 years, Goldman Sachs has increased its dividend 4 times on a year-over-year basis for an average annual increase of 22.04%. Looking ahead, future dividend growth will be dependent on earnings growth and payout ratio, which is the proportion of a company's annual earnings per share that it pays out as a dividend. Goldman's current payout ratio is 31%, meaning it paid out 31% of its trailing 12-month EPS as dividend. Earnings growth looks solid for GS for this fiscal year. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for 2026 is $57.86 per share, with earnings expected to increase 12.74% from the year ago period. Investors like dividends for many reasons; they greatly improve stock investing profits, decrease overall portfolio risk, and carry tax advantages, among others. But, not every company offers a quarterly payout. Big, established firms that have more secure profits are often seen as the best dividend options, but it's fairly uncommon to see high-growth businesses or tech start-ups offer their stockholders a dividend. During periods of rising interest rates, income investors must be mindful that high-yielding stocks tend to struggle. With that in mind, GS is a compelling investment opportunity. Not only is it a strong dividend play, but the stock currently sits at a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold). Want the latest recommendations from Zacks Investment Research? title: Here's Why We Think Goldman Sachs Group (NYSE:GS) Is Well Worth Watching - 富途牛牛
content: For beginners, it can seem like a good idea (and an exciting prospect) to buy a company that tells a good story to investors, even if it currently lacks a track record of revenue and profit.  Sometimes these stories can cloud the minds of investors, leading them to invest with their emotions rather than on the merit of good company fundamentals.  A loss-making company is yet to prove itself with profit, and eventually the inflow of external capital may dry up. If this kind of company isn't your style, you like companies that generate revenue, and even earn profits, then you may well be interested in Goldman Sachs Group (NYSE:GS). While this doesn't necessarily speak to whether it's undervalued, the profitability of the business is enough to warrant some appreciation - especially if its growing. This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality. Generally, companies experiencing growth in earnings per share (EPS) should see similar trends in share price.  That makes EPS growth an attractive quality for any company.   It certainly is nice to see that Goldman Sachs Group has managed to grow EPS by 20% per year over three years.   As a general rule, we'd say that if a company can keep up that sort of growth, shareholders will be beaming. It's often helpful to take a look at earnings before interest and tax (EBIT) margins, as well as revenue growth, to get another take on the quality of the company's growth.   It's noted that Goldman Sachs Group's revenue from operations was lower than its revenue in the last twelve months, so that could distort our analysis of its margins.     Goldman Sachs Group maintained stable EBIT margins over the last year, all while growing revenue 14% to US$59b.  That's encouraging news for the company! In the chart below, you can see how the company has grown earnings and revenue, over time.  For finer detail, click on the image. Of course the knack is to find stocks that have their best days in the future, not in the past. You could base your opinion on past performance, of course, but you may also want to check this interactive graph of professional analyst EPS forecasts for Goldman Sachs Group. We would not expect to see insiders owning a large percentage of a US$265b company like Goldman Sachs Group.  But we are reassured by the fact they have invested in the company.     Indeed, they have a considerable amount of wealth invested in it, currently valued at US$1.2b.   While that is a lot of skin in the game, we note this holding only totals to 0.4% of the business, which is a result of the company being so large.  This should still be a great incentive for management to maximise shareholder value. If you believe that share price follows earnings per share you should definitely be delving further into Goldman Sachs Group's strong EPS growth.   This EPS growth rate is something the company should be proud of, and so it's no surprise that insiders are holding on to a considerable chunk of shares.  On the balance of its merits, solid EPS growth and company insiders who are aligned with the shareholders would indicate a business that is worthy of further research.     It's still necessary to consider the ever-present spectre of investment risk.   We've identified 2 warning signs  with Goldman Sachs Group , and understanding these should be part of your investment process. There's always the possibility of doing well buying stocks that are not growing earnings and do not have insiders buying shares. But for those who consider these important metrics, we encourage you to check out companies that do have those features. title: In the wild: A timeline of techie GS Sharanya's survival in Karnataka forest - India Today
content: GS Sharanya, the 36-year-old techie who lost her way during a trek to the Tadiandamol peak in Karnataka on April 2, has been rescued after a four-day operation across the dense forests of Kodagu in Karnataka. More than the rescue operation, the story of her four-day-long survival in the wild seems straight out of a Hollywood survival film. Sharanya, who lives in Kerala's Kozhikode, has safely been reunited with her family. The 36-year-old techie narrated her survival story to reporters. Here's a day-to-day breakdown of her ordeal. Sharanya got separated from her group, which included 15 others and a nature guide, while descending the Tadiandamol peak. She had taken a wrong turn while looking for the rest of the group. With the forest too thick to navigate, she kept walking downhill until around 6:45 pm, hoping to find people. However, she found no one. She managed to call Yadhu, her office colleague, and asked him to alert others. However, her phone ran out of battery while trying to send a message to a helpline number. Sharanya decided to stay put for the night near a stream. Sharanya did not move much in the morning due to pain in her leg following hours of walking. As the visibility was good, she climbed to a higher point on the hill, hoping drones would be able to spot her. She survived by drinking water from a nearby stream. She also ensured being in relatively open areas where the search team could locate her easily. She would also scream from a higher point, expecting some response from the locals. On the third day, she planned to continue walking in search of help. Despite the threat of wild elephants in the area, she continued walking undeterred. In fact, Sharanya did not encounter any wild animals during her four-day ordeal. Later that night, heavy rain left her completely soaked and the cold night made it impossible to sleep. On the final day, Sharanya waited until around noon, still wet from the previous night's rain. While the stream took care of drinking water, her supplies had run out quickly. It was at this juncture that people from a local tribe came to her rescue. A member of a local tribe heard her screams from a distance and came to her rescue, ending her ordeal. Back home in Nadapuram on Sunday night, she was received with relief and emotion. Her mother, who had waited through days of uncertainty, broke down as she spoke of an unshaken belief in her daughter's return. For Sharanya, the forest was not just a test of survival, but an experience that has strengthened her further. When asked about her next hiking plans, she told reporters, "Maybe next summer."

title: The mother's strength was great. Gisele Silva (GS Caltes), considered the best striker in the V-Leag.. - 매일경제
content:
The mother's strength was great. Gisele Silva (GS Caltes), considered the best striker in the V-League, was crowned champion for the first time in three years. GS Caltex won the third match of the women's championship match of Jin Air 2025-26 V-League against Korea Expressway Corporation at Jangchung Gymnasium on the 5th with a set score of 3-1. As a result, GS Caltex became the champion with three wins in the championship game. GS Caltex, which achieved the women's first treble (KOVO Cup + regular league + championship game) in the 2020-21 season, topped the spring volleyball tournament for the first time in five years. This is the fourth time in history that the third-place team has won the regular league, and the first time that the third-place team has won the championship in a victory. Of course, the top contributor to the historical victory is the silver lining. Silva, who joined GS Caltex in 2023, is playing as the best striker in the women's team. He is at the top of all offensive indicators and has scored more than 1,000 points for three consecutive seasons. In this season's regular league, he was responsible for half of the team's power with an offensive share of 47.33%. As a result, GS Caltex coach Lee Young-taek was deeply concerned about "all-out volleyball," but he won six consecutive games, following the semi-playoff (single play) and the playoff (two wins out of three) in the short-term volleyball tournament. Silva also responded to the team's trust and scored more than 30 points in six consecutive games. Silva, who became the V-League champion for the first time in three years, was also honored as the MVP of the championship game. After winning the championship, Silva said, "It's hard to express this feeling in one word. I am so happy that I was able to achieve my dream for three years. I'm so proud of my team," he said. Silva complained of sudden knee pain during the game, but he did not give up until the end. He caught his left knee at the end of the third set. There was a limp figure, but he jumped high again and played the role of a troubleshooter. He said, "It's a chronic pain. Everyone knows that his knee is bad, he said. "After two days of rest, he will be fine. All of the current team members are not feeling well. Each player has an injury. I didn't want to come this far and give up, and there was no reason to give up. He explained, "I also expressed my desire to fight until the end on the faces of my colleagues." Another driving force behind the victory is the presence of daughter Siana. This is Silva's first win in seven years. It is the first time since the Polish league in the 2018-19 season before Siana was born in 2020. It is a special achievement obtained by both parenting and volleyball after childbirth. Moreover, on this day, Siana stepped out as the first pitchman to inspire her mother to win the championship. He crossed the net before the game, drawing joy and surprise from fans. Silva also smiled brightly as he looked at his daughter. Silva looked happier than ever at Sianna's story. "I practiced with Siana, but I couldn't get over the net at first. It was like that until yesterday. It would not have been easy to cross the net in front of a large crowd, but it was amazing. I'm 100% talented in volleyball." Everyone says Silva drove GS Caltex's victory, but he gave all the credit to coach Lee Young-taek and the coaching staff. He said, "Coach Lee Young-taek and the coaching staff helped me a lot in taking care of my body. Players cannot climb on their own until they get on the court. It needs a lot of people's help and effort. Without the delicate management of the team, I would not have been able to handle the tight schedule," he said. GS Caltex is set to catch Silva following last season. Director Lee also said, "I will have a lot of conversations with Silva." Silva said, "There are no plans to retire. "I can play for another two to three years," he said. "It's hard to answer the remaining questions right away." title: GS Caltex partners with Veolia to innovate Yeosu plant operations, strengthening ESG competitiveness - The Korea Post
content: GS Caltex has entered into a strategic partnership with Veolia to drive operational innovation at its Yeosu plant and enhance its sustainability management. The company announced on April 5 that it had signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) with Veolia to improve efficiency in energy and infrastructure operations while strengthening its eco-friendly competitiveness. Headquartered in France, Veolia is a global leader in water, waste, and energy management solutions. The partnership was forged on the occasion of French President Emmanuel Macron’s state visit to South Korea. It marks an expansion of the two companies’ cooperation beyond water treatment into the joint development of integrated solutions encompassing environmental, energy, and digital sectors. Under the agreement, the two companies will pursue a “Triple-X” strategy that integrates Business Transformation (BX), AI and Digital Transformation (AX), and Green Transformation (GX) in response to tightening environmental regulations and growing demands for energy efficiency. In the BX domain, GS Caltex plans to optimize and integrate wastewater treatment facilities at its Yeosu plant while exploring synergies through utility collaboration with neighboring industrial players. On the AX front, the company will adopt Veolia’s AI-based solutions to analyze utility operation data in real time and establish an intelligent process management system. In the GX area, the partners aim to enhance resource efficiency and environmental performance by applying eco-friendly technologies such as wastewater reuse, cooling system improvements, and recovery of valuable materials at the Yeosu facility. “Collaboration with Veolia will serve as a key turning point in innovating overall utility operations and advancing our ESG management,” said Huh Seh-hong, Vice Chairman of GS Caltex. “Leveraging global-level technologies and operational expertise, we will continue to strengthen our sustainable competitiveness.”

title: GS Caltex Signs Strategic Partnership with Veolia, the World's Leading Environmental Solutions Company - GS칼텍스 미디어허브
content:

                                    2026.04.06 GS Caltex (Board Chair & CEO, GS Caltex / Vice Chairman, GS Group, Saehong Hur) announced that it had signed a comprehensive memorandum of understanding (MOU) with Veolia, a global environmental solutions company, on April 3 to innovate utility operations and strengthen sustainability at its Yeosu Complex. The two companies signed the MOU following the visit of French President Emmanuel Macron to Korea, expanding their relationship from a limited, project-based collaboration in water treatment to a broader partnership aimed at sharing future strategies. Headquartered in Paris, France, Veolia is the world’s foremost company in water, waste, and energy management. To address tightening environmental regulations and growing demands for energy efficiency, the partnership will focus on developing and implementing integrated solutions across environmental, energy, and digital sectors. This will be pursued through the Triple-X framework, encompassing Business Transformation (BX), Digital & AI Transformation (DAX), and Green Transformation (GX). Under BX, the partnership will explore ways to integrate and optimize wastewater treatment operations at the Yeosu Complex as well as opportunities to enhance utility synergies with nearby companies. These efforts are expected to maximize operational efficiency and bolster competitiveness. DAX, a key strategic priority for GS Caltex, will see the introduction of Veolia’s AI-based digital solutions at the Yeosu Complex to refine real-time analysis of utility operations data and improve intelligent process management systems. By leveraging these capabilities, GS Caltex will accelerate the transition to a smart plant through process optimization, anomaly detection, and predictive maintenance. For GX, focused on sustainability, GS Caltex will collaborate with Veolia to adopt the latter’s proprietary eco-friendly technologies at the Yeosu Complex, including wastewater reuse, cooling system upgrades, and resource recovery. The approach is intended to reduce carbon emissions and solidify the foundation for a circular economy. CEO Saehong Hur said, “Our collaboration with Veolia will serve as a catalyst for transforming our overall utility operations and elevating our ESG management to the next level,” adding, “We will strengthen our sustainable competitiveness based on world-class technology and operational expertise.” [About Veolia]Headquartered in Paris, France, Veolia has maintained its position as the world’s foremost company in water, waste, and energy management for over 170 years since its establishment in 1853. As of 2025, the company reported revenues of KRW 75.9 trillion and employs approximately 215,000 people worldwide. title: GS Retail is expected to continue its profitability improvement this year as consumer sentiment reco.. - 매일경제
content:
GS Retail is expected to continue its profitability improvement this year as consumer sentiment recovers and the convenience store industry's intensive restructuring is combined. In response, Hanwha Investment & Securities raised its performance forecast and target stock price of GS Retail. Hanwha Investment & Securities raised its target stock price of GS Retail from 27,000 won to 30,000 won in a report published on the 6th. GS Retail's stock price has rebounded more than 50% in the past year, reaching 21,450 won (as of the closing price on the 3rd). According to Hanwha Investment & Securities, GS Retail's consolidated sales in the first quarter of this year are expected to rise 2.9% year-on-year to KRW 2.84 trillion and its operating profit to KRW 50 billion, a 29.6% jump, in line with market expectations. In particular, while maintaining a conservative opening stance in the convenience store sector, which is its main business, and suppressing the increase in fixed costs, the growth rate of existing stores is expected to improve significantly, recording 4%. In addition, the quality growth of surviving stores is expected as the closure of sluggish stores continues in the convenience store industry. According to the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy, the number of convenience store stores fell 2.9% in February compared to the same month last year. While lower-tier operators are closing sluggish stores, sales per store of the top-tier stores that survived are on the rise. Lee Jin-hyeop, a researcher at Hanwha Investment & Securities, said, "With quantitative growth limited, qualitative growth is expected to continue and the leverage effect of convenience store operations is expected to begin in earnest. This is the path that Japanese convenience stores walked 10 years ago, and the stock price of Japanese convenience stores was reevaluated at the time." It is predicted that competitors will continue to close their sluggish stores. Researcher Lee said, "Korea Seven's sales fell 9% last year compared to the previous year, but its operating deficit has been reduced, but it is highly likely to maintain its existing strategy as its annual operating deficit still reaches 68.6 billion won. E-Mart 24 is also in a conservative situation." In addition to convenience stores, other business units that have been hampering their performance are also gradually narrowing their deficits through the efficiency process. Super sectors such as GS the Fresh are also expected to see their existing store growth turn positive in the first quarter of this year. ※ This article was translated using AI technology for reader convenience. title: GS Engineering Sells 1,780 Units Near Daejeon's Yonggye Station - 조선일보
content: GS Engineering & Construction's Doan Jai Centum Rich offers proximity to future Yonggye Station and expanding infrastructure in Doan New Town GS Engineering & Construction is proceeding with the sale of ‘Doan Jai Centum Rich,’ a residential complex being built in Yonggye-dong 267-3, Yuseong-gu, Daejeon. The complex spans from basement level 2 to a maximum of 42 floors above ground and will consist of 2,293 households, with 1,780 units available for general sale. Unit 1 comprises 841 households of 84㎡ and 105 households of 99㎡. Unit 2 includes 626 households of 84㎡, 206 households of 115㎡, and 2 households of 134㎡. Over 80% of the total units are 84㎡, a size in high demand. Doan Jai Centum Rich will be supplied as the second-phase development of Doan New Town nears completion. Doan New Town, a large-scale residential district spanning Seo-gu and Yuseong-gu in Daejeon, has seen rising demand for housing as key infrastructure was established through its first and second phases. Once the third-phase development is completed, it is expected to become a representative emerging residential area in southwestern Daejeon. Doan Jai Centum Rich is located near the planned Yonggye Station of Daejeon Urban Railway Line 2 Tram, accessible within a 5-minute walk. Once the line opens in 2028, travel to Yuseong Hot Springs Station will take approximately 10 minutes, and to Government Complex Station, around 21 minutes. In addition to the tram line, the expansion of the Fourth Ring Road is planned, which is expected to significantly improve regional accessibility once the project is fully underway. Educational facilities are also well-established. An elementary school and high school are planned near Unit 1, while a middle school is under development around Unit 2. Residents will also have access to Lotte Mart, NC Department Store, Lotte Cinema, and Konyang University Hospital. A central park spanning approximately 350,000㎡ is set to be built south of the complex. As nearby development projects progress, living infrastructure is expected to continue expanding. In March, the Southwest Comprehensive Sports Town broke ground in Hakha-dong, Yuseong-gu, on a 760,000㎡ site with an investment of 1.4 trillion Korean won. The project includes sports facilities, parks, public institutions, and residential areas, alongside plans for a national semiconductor industrial complex. Doan Jai Centum Rich incorporates specialized designs reflecting current trends. It adopts a 4-bay slab-type structure (aligning the living room and three bedrooms in the same direction) and is primarily oriented south to optimize lighting and ventilation. Alpha rooms and dressing rooms enhance space utility, while piloti designs on the first floor of each building ensure openness and privacy for low-level households. Community facilities are diverse. The ‘Club Jai-an,’ an exclusive space for residents, includes a golf practice range, fitness club, sauna, cafeteria, GX room, library, and guesthouse. Childcare facilities and a kids’ library are also available. A source from the sales department stated, “Doan Jai Centum Rich is a large-scale Jai-brand complex supplied in the core area of Doan New Town. As the last major sale in the second phase, it holds rarity and is expected to become a central residential hub in the region once the third-phase development is completed.” The model house for Doan Jai Centum Rich will be located at 667-6, Yonggye-dong, Yuseong-gu, Daejeon.

title: GS Caltex Claims Undefeated Title, Historic Third-Place Victory - 조선일보
content: Defeating Heungkuk Life, Hyundai Engineering & Construction, Korea Expressway Corporation in six straight postseason matches, ending five-year title drought [OSEN=Hong Ji-su Reporter] GS Caltex, which finished third in the regular season, achieved an 'undefeated' championship in the postseason. GS Caltex won the 3rd match of the 2025-2026 V-League Women's Division Championship Decider held at Jangchung Gymnasium on the 5th with a set score of 3-1 (25-15, 19-25, 25-20, 25-20). Silva scored 36 points, Kwon Min-ji 15 points, and Oh Se-yeon 11 points. Korea Expressway Corporation’s Moma scored 18 points and 15 points but was no match for GS Caltex’s firepower. GS Caltex, which advanced to the championship decider by defeating Heungkuk Life in the preliminary playoffs and Hyundai Engineering & Construction in the playoffs, ascended to the top without conceding a single match to Korea Expressway Corporation. GS Caltex played a total of six matches from the preliminary playoffs to the championship decider without a single loss. In the preliminary playoffs against Heungkuk Life on the 24th of last month, they secured a 3-1 set score victory and advanced to the playoffs. In the 1st playoff match against Hyundai Engineering & Construction on the 25th, they won 3-1, and in the 2nd match on the 28th, they won 3-0, successfully advancing to the championship decider. They also won all matches against Korea Expressway Corporation. In the 1st match, they won 3-1, and in the 2nd match on the 3rd, they won 3-2. GS Caltex concluded the series in the 3rd match. Unlike Korea Expressway Corporation, which advanced directly to the championship decider as the regular league leader, GS Caltex played their 6th postseason match on that day. Physical stamina was a variable, but the GS Caltex team, riding high on momentum, overcame the physical challenges. They reclaimed the title for the first time in five years since the 2020-2021 season. GS Caltex has now lifted their fourth championship trophy in the women’s professional volleyball championship decider, following the 2007-2008, 2013-2014, and 2020-2021 seasons. Starting from 3rd place, GS Caltex set a historic record as the first team to start from the preliminary playoffs and win the championship decider, as well as the first 3rd-place team to achieve an undefeated championship.

title: GS Caltex Wins Undefeated Championship, Silva Named MVP - 조선일보
content: Silva's 36.3 average, 47%+ leads GS Caltex to first title in five years, undefeated GS Caltex won 3-1 (25-15 19-25 25-20 25-20) in the third match of the 2025-2026 V-League Women’s Division Championship Finals held at Jangchung Gymnasium on the 5th. Silva scored 36 points, Kwon Minji 15 points, and Oh Se-yeon 11 points. Silva, who topped the regular season in scoring, continued her strong performance in the postseason. Appearing in all six postseason games, she averaged 36.3 points, proving her worth as the ‘Cuba Express’ and was selected as MVP. She received 33 out of 34 votes (1 abstention) in the press corps poll, effectively a unanimous decision. She scored over 40 points twice and recorded over 30 points in all six games. Additionally, Silva’s attack success rate never dropped below 47%. In the third match, she scored 36 points with a 47.89% attack success rate. GS Caltex, which defeated Heungkuk Life in the quarterfinals and Hyundai Engineering & Construction in the semifinals, advanced to the championship finals without losing a single game to Korea Expressway Corporation. From the quarterfinals to the championship finals, GS Caltex played six games and remained undefeated. On the 24th of last month, they secured a 3-1 victory over Heungkuk Life in the quarterfinals and advanced to the semifinals. In the first semifinal against Hyundai Engineering & Construction on the 25th, they won 3-1, and in the second semifinal on the 28th, they won 3-0, successfully advancing to the finals. They also won all matches against Korea Expressway Corporation. In the first match on the 1st, they won 3-1, and in the second match on the 3rd, they won 3-2, concluding the series in the third match. During this process, Silva scored 42 points with a 59.15% attack success rate against Heungkuk Life. In the first semifinal against Hyundai Engineering & Construction, she scored 40 points (49.65% attack success rate), and in the second semifinal, she scored 32 points (49.09% attack success rate). In the first match against Korea Expressway Corporation on the 1st, she scored 33 points (49.21% attack success rate), and in the second match, she scored 35 points (47.69% attack success rate), leading GS Caltex to an undefeated championship. Unlike Korea Expressway Corporation, which advanced directly to the finals as the regular league leader, GS Caltex played their sixth postseason game up to that day. Physical stamina was a concern, but the GS Caltex team overcame the challenges. They reclaimed the title for the first time in five years since the 2020-2021 season. GS Caltex has now won their fourth championship trophy in the Women’s Professional Volleyball Championship Finals, following their victories in the 2007-2008, 2013-2014, and 2020-2021 seasons. Starting from third place, GS Caltex set a historic record as the first team to win the championship finals after starting in the quarterfinals and the first third-place team to achieve an undefeated championship. [OSEN=Hong Jisu Reporter] GS Caltex, which ranked third in the regular season, achieved an undefeated postseason championship. ‘Cupa Express’ Gisele Silva became the MVP.

title: Third-Place GS Caltex Wins Women’s V-League Championship Undefeated - 조선일보
content: Foreign star's 1,301 points and team's historic postseason run secure first title in five years On the 5th, the third game of the V-League Women’s Championship Finals between GS Caltex and Korea Expressway Corporation was held at Jangchung Gymnasium in Seoul. Before the match, Gyselle Silva (35), a foreign player for GS Caltex, and her husband and daughter Siana (6) stood on the court. Silva, who was warming up, smiled warmly and kissed her daughter’s cheek. Once the match began, Silva’s “motherly smile” vanished. With a cold gaze, she focused intently on the game, remaining expressionless whether scoring or conceding points. Only after securing the final point that clinched the team’s victory in the fourth set did Silva raise her arms and grin brightly. GS Caltex defeated Korea Expressway Corporation 3-1 in sets (25-15, 19-25, 25-20, 25-20) and claimed the season’s championship. Ranked fifth at the start of the fifth round, GS Caltex fought back with an 8-4 record to finish third in the regular league. They then swept six consecutive postseason games: a single-match playoff against fourth-place Heungkuk Life, a best-of-three playoff against second-place Hyundai Engineering & Construction, and a best-of-five championship series against first-place Korea Expressway Corporation, completing a perfect “upset victory.” This marked GS Caltex’s first championship in five years since their integrated title win in 2021. It was the fourth time a third-place team won the championship, following GS Caltex in 2008, Heungkuk Life in 2009, and Korea Expressway Corporation in 2023. However, GS Caltex became the first third-place team to achieve an undefeated postseason run to the title. At the heart of the victory was the “Cuban ace” Silva. Despite criticism of GS Caltex’s heavy reliance on her—sparking jokes that “GS” might stand for Gyselle Silva—the team entrusted her with nearly half of their attacks. Silva’s attack share in the regular league was 43.03%. She played all 36 regular-season games, relentlessly spiking with ironclad stamina, and shattered the single-season scoring record for the women’s division with 1,083 points. In her third year in the domestic league, she also became the first player to record over 1,000 points for three consecutive seasons. Silva continued her dominance in the playoffs. She scored over 30 points in six straight games, finishing the season with a record 1,301 points across the regular league and postseason. She was voted the Championship Finals MVP with 33 out of 34 votes (1 abstention) in a field reporter poll. When asked about her future with the team, Silva replied, “It’s not my decision.” GS Caltex Vice Chairman Hur Sae-hong, the team owner, responded, “We promise to renew her contract,” and fans chanted Silva’s name in celebration. Domestic players like Kwon Min-ji and Yoo Seo-yeon also shared the burden by supporting Silva’s attacks. Setter Ahn Hye-jin gained stability as the season progressed, laying the foundation for the championship. Korea Expressway Corporation, who dominated the regular league with an overwhelming first-place finish, crumbled against GS Caltex. The team’s sudden split with head coach Kim Jong-min, who led them for a decade, created turmoil ahead of the championship. Additionally, foreign star Moma Basoko (Cameroon) underperformed, contributing to their three straight losses.

title: GS Caltex Reclaims V-League Title After Five-Year Gap - 조선일보
content: Silva's 36 Points Lead Team to Championship Victory Over Korea Expressway Corporation [OSEN=Hong Ji-su Reporter] GS Caltex, which finished 3rd in the regular season, concluded the season at the highest position. GS Caltex won 3-1 (25-15 19-25 25-20 25-20) in sets in the 3rd match of the 2025-2026 V-League women’s championship decider held at Jangchung Gymnasium on the 5th. They reclaimed the top spot for the first time in five years since the 2020-21 season. Silva scored 36 points, Kwon Min-ji 15 points, and Oh Se-yeon 11 points. Korea Expressway Corporation’s Moma scored 18 points and 15 points, but they were no match for GS Caltex’s firepower. GS Caltex, which defeated Heungkuk Life in the semifinals and Hyundai Engineering & Construction in the playoffs, ascended to the championship decider without losing a single match to Korea Expressway Corporation. GS Caltex, which won 3-1 in the 1st match and 3-2 in the 2nd match on the 3rd, concluded the series in the 3rd match. In the 1st set, GS Caltex took the first score when Korea Expressway Corporation’s Kim Sa-bin’s quick attack went out. Silva’s quick open attack then made it 2-0. Thanks to the performances of Kwon Min-ji and Silva, GS Caltex took the lead. Although they faced a reversal due to Kang So-hwi’s unstoppable attacks, they turned the game around with Oh Se-yeon’s blocking and quick attack, Silva’s quick open attack, and another block from Oh Se-yeon. The momentum continued with Silva and Kwon Min-ji’s performances, shaking off Korea Expressway Corporation’s pursuit. The 2nd set was fiercely contested. Silva’s open attack and blocking gave them an early lead, but they began to trail as they failed to contain Moma, Bae Yu-na, and Thanacha. In the latter half, they fell behind due to Moma and Kim Sa-bin, resulting in a 1-1 set score. In the 3rd set, GS Caltex remained locked in a close battle until Moma’s strong serve and Thanacha’s quick open attack put them at 3-5. However, Kwon Min-ji, Choi Ga-eun, and Silva stepped up to tie it at 6-6. The score reached 8-8, and after Korea Expressway Corporation’s Lee Yoon-jung’s serve went out, Silva’s successful attack made it 10-8. Oh Se-yeon’s block and Kwon Min-ji’s point extended the lead to 12-8. Korea Expressway Corporation’s counterattack led by Thanacha and Moma was fierce, but Silva bombarded them. Silva’s three consecutive points secured the 3rd set for GS Caltex. In the early stages of the 4th set, Kwon Min-ji and Choi Ga-eun shone, while Korea Expressway Corporation countered with Moma and Thanacha. However, Lee Yoon-jung’s unforced error and Moma’s attack out widened the gap. GS Caltex pulled away with points from Silva and Yu Seo-yeon. At 15-11, Yu Seo-yeon’s open attack, block, and quick open attack in succession extended the lead to 18-11. The momentum shifted decisively to GS Caltex. At 20-14, Silva scored, followed by Oh Se-yeon’s block. Although Silva’s attack was blocked by Kang So-hwi and Thanacha, narrowing the gap, Kwon Min-ji’s consecutive points decided the championship decider winner.

title: GS Caltex teams up with France's Veolia on smart, green plant push - The Korea Herald
content:
Business GS Caltex teams up with France's Veolia on smart, green plant push GS Caltex has formed a strategic partnership with France’s Veolia to upgrade utility operations and strengthen sustainability at its Yeosu plant, the company said Sunday. The agreement, signed during French President Emmanuel Macron’s visit to Korea, marks a shift from project-based cooperation to a broader, long-term partnership. Under the deal, the two companies will jointly develop integrated solutions across environmental, energy and digital domains to meet tightening regulations and rising demand for efficiency. The collaboration will be structured under a “Triple-X” framework — business transformation (BX), digital and AI transformation (DAX), and green transformation (GX). In BX, the partners will focus on optimizing wastewater treatment at the Yeosu plant and exploring utility synergies with nearby industrial sites to improve operational efficiency. For DAX, GS Caltex will adopt Veolia’s AI-based solutions to enable real-time data analysis and advanced process control, supporting a shift toward a smart plant through predictive maintenance and anomaly detection. In GX, the companies will deploy eco-friendly technologies, such as wastewater reuse, cooling system optimization and resource recovery to cut emissions and strengthen circular economy practices. “The partnership with Veolia marks a key step in transforming our utility operations and advancing ESG management,” GS Caltex Vice Chairman Hur Sae-hong said. “We aim to build sustainable competitiveness based on world-class technologies and operational expertise.” Samsung posts record W57tr profit, crushes estimates on AI memory boom Samsung Electronics posted record-breaking quarterly earnings that far exceeded market expectations, driven by surging demand for AI memory chips. title: Becky G’s See-Through Naked Wardrobe Lace Top Hangs on by a Few Hooks - Reality Tea
content: Becky G is turning heads with her fashion choices for her latest music video, donning a see-through lace top hanging by a few hooks and a pair of leggings for the “Marathon” MV. The singer wore a bold, head-to-toe red ensemble that perfectly blends street style with high-fashion risk-taking. Known for her fearless approach to trends, Becky G’s newest look is a masterclass in monochrome styling. It captures a daring, modern aesthetic that is already sparking conversations across the fashion world and social media. The centerpiece of Becky G’s striking look is a vibrant red stretch lace cardigan top from Naked Wardrobe. The top features a delicate, see-through floral lace pattern that offers a hint of skin while maintaining a structured silhouette. Adding to the drama, the cardigan is held together by just a few small hooks at the center, creating a daring peekaboo effect. This barely-there fastening gives the outfit a sense of effortless, edgy movement that works perfectly for a music video setting. She paired the top with matching red lace leggings, also from Naked Wardrobe. These leggings carry the same intricate floral motif and sheerness, creating a seamless and bold monochrome aesthetic. To round out the ensemble, Becky G wore red pointed-toe heels with buckle details and carried a sleek black shoulder bag. Her long, dark hair was styled in soft, voluminous waves that draped over her shoulders, adding a touch of classic glamor to the trendy outfit. Becky G looked absolutely radiant and confident while filming on location. Her makeup remained polished with a sharp winged liner and a neutral lip, ensuring the focus stayed on the fiery red attire. The overall look is a perfect representation of her signature style: fearless, feminine, and always ahead of the curve. Originally reported by Samridhi Goel on theFashionSpot.
